decisions.reasoning: The game just started, and the robber is on the brick tile, so no resources will be produced this turn. Building a settlement is the best option to start gaining resources. Node 28 produces wood, which is the most valuable resource for the early game. It also has a 3:1 port, which is useful for trading.

The game just started, and I need to establish my first road to connect to my settlement. Both options (28, 29) and (27, 28) are valid, but (28, 29) is closer to my settlement, making it easier to build more structures in the future. I choose to build the road here.

The game just started, and the opponent already has 1 VP. I need to quickly establish settlements to collect resources. Settlements 28 and 29 are adjacent to each other and produce resources that I can use to build roads and settlements. Settlement 28 is already occupied by the opponent, so I should place my settlement at node 29, which produces BRICK and WHEAT.

The game just started, and the opponent has a settlement at node 28. To counteract their early move, I should build a road connected to my settlement at node 31. Building a road at (30, 31) is the best option as it connects directly to my settlement and starts building my road network.

The robber is on a brick tile, so rolling a 7 will stop brick production. Building a settlement on a brick-producing tile is crucial to securing a steady supply of bricks. Node 28 is a good choice as it is close to the initial road and produces brick on 9 and 10, which are common rolls. The 2:1 brick port at node 52 is also beneficial.
First, I need to build roads to connect my settlements. The road between nodes 0 and 1 is the best option as it connects my second settlement and allows me to expand further.

Given that the opponent has 2 cities and 2 settlements for a total of 2 VP, and I only have 1 VP from my settlement, I need to build more settlements and potentially a city to catch up. The best option is to build a settlement at node 28, which is connected to my existing road and is on a 3:1 port for wheat, giving a good resource advantage. This move also helps in blocking potential opponent settlements.

Need to expand road network and get closer to opponent's settlements to block them. Building road (24, 25) connects to my existing road and gets me closer to blocking the opponent's road between nodes 0 and 1.
The robber is on a sheep-producing tile, so trading sheep for another resource is beneficial. The 2:1 sheep port on node 25 is the best option, as it allows for efficient resource acquisition.

The robber is currently on a wheat-producing tile, so moving it will stop wheat production. The opponent has a settlement on the wheat tile at (-2, 0, 2), so moving the robber there will steal from the opponent. No other tile has as many pips, so this is the best move.

The robber is currently on (2, -2, 0), which is an ORE tile. Moving the robber away from this tile will prevent the opponent from stealing resources from it. The opponent's longest road is currently 2, so moving the robber to a tile that could potentially disrupt their road is beneficial. Moving to (1, -2, 1) would be a good choice as it is close to the opponent's road and could potentially split it.

Blue needs to expand and secure resources. Building a road to connect to the sheep port at node 26 would be beneficial. The sheep port offers a 2:1 trade rate, and securing this port could provide a steady stream of resources.

Need to build a road to connect to the port at node 32 for a 3:1 trade. No immediate settlement or city placement is possible. The other road options do not connect to any ports or are not strategic for resource collection.

Blue needs to expand and gain resources. The settlements on nodes 1 and 31 are already producing. Building a road on (6, 23) connects to the existing settlement at node 31 and allows for potential future settlements and cities. This is the most strategic option for resource gathering and road expansion.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and the only resource I have in hand is sheep. Trading sheep for brick is the best option to get a new resource and potentially improve my position. The 2:1 port at node 52 and 53 provides a good rate for this trade.
End turn to avoid playing a development card now, as no immediate benefits are seen. No ports or trades offer better deals.
Need to build to increase resources and VP. Blue has a settlement at 31, so building at 23 would allow access to BRICK, SHEEP, and WOOD resources. No immediate need for ports or trading.
The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have 4 brick cards. Trading 4 bricks for 1 wheat is a good deal at 4:1. No other development cards or building actions are beneficial at this moment.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile. Moving the robber to a WHEAT-producing tile could be beneficial. The tile at (2, -2, 0) produces WHEAT on 4 and 8, which are common rolls. Moving the robber there would also steal from the opponent, who has 2 VP and is close to winning. This is the most strategic move.

End the turn as you have enough resources to build a road but no immediate strategic benefit. Your longest road is already 3, and building a road now wouldn't extend it. You have no development cards to play, and no immediate need for resources through trading or other actions.
End turn to avoid discarding resources; no immediate benefit from building roads or buying a development card.

Need to build roads to expand and get more resource production. Road between nodes 6 and 7 is the only one that doesn't conflict with existing roads and allows access to new resources. No immediate need to buy a development card or trade resources.

Blue needs to build to gain more VP. The settlement at node 7 would give Blue 3 resources (brick, sheep, and wood) which is better than just a road. No development cards are available yet, so building is the best option.

Need to build a road to expand and potentially block the opponent. The road at (0, 20) is a strategic choice as it connects to an intersection with a sheep port and could block the opponent's potential expansion.

End the turn as there are no immediate building opportunities that would significantly improve my position. The opponent has a small lead in VP and roads, but no immediate threat. No development cards are available to play, and the resources in hand are not enough to build anything.

I need to expand my network and secure resources. Building a road to node 23 (action [11]) connects to my existing settlement at node 7 and a port at node 32, which can help me get wood and sheep. This is a strategic move that supports my resource collection and road expansion.

The opponent has a significant lead in VP and longest road. Trading wood for brick is not beneficial since the opponent has excess brick. Trading wood for wheat or ore could be useful, but the opponent has excess sheep. Trading wood for sheep could help reduce the opponent's advantage in Largest Army. Therefore, the best option is to trade wood for sheep.

The opponent has a longer road and more VP. I need to build to gain VP and road length. The sheep port offers the best trade rate, and I have 2 sheep in hand. Trading 2 sheep for 1 ore or wheat would help build a settlement or road. Trading for wood or brick is less beneficial due to lower port rates.

Need resources to build. Blue has 4 VP and needs 6 more, while Red has 2 VP and needs 8 more. Blue's longest road is 4 and Red's is 1. Blue should focus on building a city to increase VP. The BRICK port at node 52/53 can be used to get BRICKs, which are needed for roads and cities. No immediate action is needed as trading 4 BRICKs for 1 WHEAT is not beneficial.
Need to reduce hand size due to 7 being rolled. Brick is the only resource in hand, and discarding it will allow me to take an action.
Need to reduce hand size due to the 7 roll rule; brick is the only resource in excess. Discarding brick will not impact settlement building or resource collection significantly.
Need to discard to avoid excess resources. Red has more VP and longer road, so avoid giving them resources. Brick is most abundant in hand and won't trigger discard rule.
Need to discard to avoid forced discard due to 7 roll. Brick and ore are more valuable, but sheep is in excess and can be traded easily.

The robber is currently on the BRICK tile, which produces when the dice add up to 9 or 10. Moving the robber away could prevent resource theft. The opponent has a large road network, particularly in the BRICK-producing area, so moving the robber to their BRICK-producing tile (1, 0, 0) would be strategic. No development cards are available to play, and the hand does not contain any resources to trade or discard.

The robber is currently on a brick-producing tile, which produces on 9 and 10. Moving the robber away from this tile will prevent the opponent from stealing resources. The opponent has a settlement at node 25, which is two edges away from the current robber position. Moving the robber to node (2, -2, 0) will prevent the opponent from stealing from the brick-producing tile.

The robber is currently on (-2, 2, 0), which is an ORE tile. Moving the robber to a WHEAT tile would prevent the opponent from producing wheat, which is valuable. The closest WHEAT tile is at (2, -2, 0) with 4 pips, so moving the robber there is the best option.

End turn is the safest option as I have no immediate need to build or play a card. The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have excess brick cards, but no immediate need to use them. No other actions offer clear benefit.

The opponent has a longer road and more VP. I need to build a settlement to gain more VP. The sheep port is the best option as it allows a 2:1 trade. Building at node 26 will produce sheep on 3, 5, and 6, which are common rolls.

End the turn to avoid the robber moving, which could disrupt production and potentially steal resources. Blue has a strong road network and no immediate need to build. The hand has 5 BRICKs, which can be traded for other resources.

End turn as there are no immediate opportunities to gain resources or points. The opponent has a smaller lead in VP and longest road, but no development cards, making it difficult for them to close the gap.
Must discard a resource as 7 was rolled and I have 7 resources. Wood is the most abundant in my hand and will not significantly impact my strategy.
Need to reduce hand size to 7 cards. Brick is the only resource in excess. Discarding brick allows more options in future turns.
Need to reduce hand size due to 7 roll. Brick is most abundant in hand. No development cards to play.
Need to reduce hand size due to the 7 roll rule; BRICK is the only resource in excess. Discarding BRICK will not impact building options or VP gain.
Need to reduce hand size due to 7 roll. Brick or ore are excess. Brick is more valuable, but ore is needed for future city building. Discarding ore is better for VP potential.

The opponent has a strong longest road (4 segments) and 4 VP, while I only have 2 VP. I need to disrupt the opponent's longest road and gain resources to build more settlements and cities. Moving the robber to (1, -1, 0) would cut off the opponent's road between nodes 0 and 1, potentially breaking their longest road. The 2:1 sheep port at node 25 is also a good option for resource gain.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and the only resource I have in hand is sheep. Trading sheep for brick is the best option to get a resource that can be used to build more settlements or cities. The 2:1 port at node 52 or 53 is the best option for this trade.

Blue needs to build roads to connect new settlements and cities, and potentially gain an advantage over the opponent. The key options are to build roads to connect to new nodes or to extend existing roads. Building road (23, 52) connects to a BRICK port, which could be beneficial for resource management.

Blue needs to build a road to expand their network and potentially block the opponent. The road at (52, 53) is a good option as it connects to a port and provides a 2:1 trade for brick, which is currently scarce.

Blue needs to build roads to connect to new settlements and cities. The best option is to build a road between nodes 29 and 33 as it connects to a port for a 3:1 trade and is not blocked by the robber.
End turn as no immediate building opportunities are available. Blue has sufficient VP and resources but needs to secure a development card to gain more VP.

Blue needs to build roads to expand and potentially block the opponent. The most strategic road placements are (24, 53) and (7, 24) as they connect to existing roads and could block the opponent's movement. Building (24, 53) is the best option as it connects to the longest road and could potentially extend it.

Blue needs to increase VP and resources. The opponent has only 2 VP and Blue has 6, so building a city or settlement is the priority. The opponent has a settlement at node 25, so building at node 35 would block their longest road and give Blue 2 VP for the city. The opponent has 1 brick, 1 ore, and 1 wheat, so taking resources from them would be beneficial. The 2:1 port at node 35 for ore would allow Blue to get 2 ore for 1 resource, which is more efficient than trading at the bank.
End turn as there are no immediate building opportunities that would significantly improve my position. The opponent has a larger lead in VP and more pieces available for building.
End turn as there are no immediate opportunities to build or gain VP. The opponent has a significant lead in VP and pieces, and there are no available development cards in the hand.

The robber is currently on (1, -1, 0), which is a brick-producing tile. Moving the robber away will prevent resource theft. The opponent has only 2 VP, so targeting them with the robber is crucial. Moving to a wheat-producing tile (45, 47) will also help in gaining resources. The best move is to move the robber to (1, -2, 1) which is a wheat-producing tile.

The robber is currently on a brick-producing tile, which is valuable to the opponent. Moving the robber away will prevent the opponent from stealing resources. The opponent has a large lead in VP and resources, so we need to prevent them from gaining more. Moving the robber to a wheat-producing tile will stop the opponent from gaining resources on a 4 or 8 roll.

End turn since there are no immediate opportunities to build or play a development card that would significantly improve my position. The opponent has a significant lead in VP and longest road, and I don't have enough resources to build anything useful.

The opponent has a significant VP lead and a long road. I need to build a settlement or road to stay competitive. Building a road is cheaper and can help me connect to more resources. The BRICK port offers a good 2:1 trade, and I have the resources for it.

I need to build a road to expand my network and potentially block the opponent's longest road. The maritime trades are not beneficial as I don't have enough sheep to make them worthwhile. The robber is on a brick-producing tile, so I should build a road to intercept it before the opponent can.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and there are multiple 2:1 sheep ports available. Trading sheep for brick or wheat would be beneficial. However, wheat is more abundant and easier to get, so it's the best option.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and since the opponent has a brick-producing settlement, rolling a 7 would steal a brick. The opponent is also close to the longest road, so securing the longest road is crucial. No development cards are available, so the best move is to end the turn.

Blue needs to build to get more VP and resources. Settlements are cheaper and faster to build compared to cities. Node 20 is a good spot for a settlement as it is connected to a road and can collect resources from multiple tiles. It also has a 3:1 port, which can be useful for future trades.
End turn as no affordable actions are available. The opponent has a significant lead in VP and pieces, and there are no immediate opportunities to gain VP or resources.

The opponent has a significant lead in VP. The best option is to build a road to connect to the settlement at 28 and gain access to the sheep port at 25, which can help in resource collection and potentially future development card purchases.

End turn as there are no valuable actions available. The opponent has a significant lead in VP and longest road, and there are no development cards or trades that would significantly improve the current situation.
Need to increase VP. Can't build a settlement or city now. Ports are not useful with current hand. Trading sheep for resources is the best option.

The opponent is close to victory with 7 VP. I need to play a knight to prevent the opponent from winning and potentially gain resources or longest road. There are no immediate settlements or cities to build that would significantly impact the game, and I don't have enough resources to build a road or trade effectively. Playing a knight is the best option to disrupt the opponent's progress.

The opponent has a road to my longest road, so I need to build a road to extend it. The best option is to build a road to node 24, which is part of my longest road and will make it longer.

The robber is currently on (2, -1, -1), producing ORE. Moving the robber to a tile producing WHEAT will be more beneficial as WHEAT is more abundant and can be traded at a better rate. The best option is to move the robber to (1, -2, 1) which is producing WHEAT on a roll of 11 (2 pips) and is accessible via the road network.

Moving the robber to tile (1, -1, 1) will stop the opponent's brick production, as it produces on 9 and 10, which are 4 and 3 pips respectively.
End turn since no resources or actions can be used to gain VP or secure resources. No development cards are available to play, and no buildings can be constructed due to lack of pieces.

The opponent has a strong longest road (9) and more VP. I need to play defensively and disrupt their longest road. Moving the robber to (1, -1, 0) will block their road at node 7, which is part of their longest road.

I need to build a road to expand my network and potentially claim the longest road bonus. The road at (26, 27) is the best option as it connects to my existing road and is not blocked by any opponent settlements or cities.
Need to build a road to extend my road network and potentially block the opponent's longest road. Building road (1, 2) is the best option as it connects to an existing road and extends the network.
No pieces to build or buy, and only 1 development card to play. END_TURN is the only option.

The robber is currently on a BRICK-producing tile (9), which is a key resource for me. I need to move the robber to a tile that produces a resource I lack, like WHEAT or ORE. Moving to a WHEAT-producing tile (8) would be ideal, but that's not possible. Moving to an ORE-producing tile (3) is the next best option.
No resources in hand, and opponent has no development cards. Building is not possible as no pieces left. The only action available is to end the turn.

Blue needs to gain resources and VP. The opponent has only 2 VP and is far behind. Blue has no development cards and needs to build a city to gain 2 VP. The opponent has a city and a longest road, but Blue can block the longest road with a settlement. Trading is not beneficial as Blue has no resources to trade. Building a road is not necessary as Blue has no pieces left.
No build options are available since all pieces are already used. The only action left is to buy a development card, which is the only way to gain additional resources or victory points.

The opponent is at 2 VP and needs 8 more to win, while I have 7 VP and need 3 more. I should play a Monopoly card to hinder the opponent's progress. The wood port is the best option as it is on a tile that produces wood (BRICK on 9) and the opponent has a settlement on a wood port (node 28). Playing a Monopoly on wood will deprive the opponent of a key resource.

The opponent has a small lead in VP and longest road. Building a road to connect to an empty intersection (node 19) would allow me to place a settlement there, increasing my VP and potentially blocking the opponent's road expansion. Node 19 is two edges away from my existing settlement at node 1, satisfying the distance rule.

The robber is on a brick-producing tile, and I have only 1 ore. Playing a knight card can move the robber and potentially disrupt the opponent's strategy. Rolling might produce resources but doesn't directly affect the game state in a strategic way.

The robber is currently on a brick-producing tile (produces on 9 and 10), and moving it off this tile will prevent the opponent from stealing resources. The opponent has a settlement on node 25, which is connected to a brick-producing tile, so moving the robber there would be most beneficial. No other development cards or actions are available, and the hand does not contain any resources to trade or buy cards.

The robber is on the BRICK tile, so the only resource available is BRICK. I need to gain more BRICKs to build cities and advance my longest road. The best option is to trade 4 SHEEP for 1 BRICK at the 2:1 port.

The robber is currently on (1, -2, 1), which is producing BRICK. Moving the robber to a tile that produces WHEAT or ORE would be beneficial, as these resources are scarce for the opponent and I have more of them. Need to discard to avoid excess resources. Wood is the most abundant in hand and not critical for immediate builds. Discarding wood prevents excess and keeps resources balanced.
Need to reduce hand size to 7 cards. Wood is the most abundant resource in hand and can be traded easily. Discarding wood will allow me to take an action.
Need to discard to avoid excess resources. Blue has 2 wheat and 1 ore, excess of wheat. Discarding wheat will not impact building options as much as discarding other resources.

Since I have no cities and only one settlement, I need to consider building a settlement or road. However, playing a knight card first gives me more options.

End turn as no build options are available and no development cards to play.
End turn since no build options are available and no immediate strategic advantage in playing a development card.

Trading at a 2:1 ratio is better than 3:1, and sheep are the most abundant resource on the board. Trading sheep for wood is a good move as it gives us a valuable resource and helps us build roads.

Need to discard to avoid having more than 7 resources. Blue has 7 wood, 3 brick, 1 sheep, 1 wheat, and 1 ore, totaling 12 resources. Must discard 5 resources. Wood is the most abundant, so discard 5 wood.

I need to discard to avoid having more than 7 resources. I have 2 wheat and 3 brick, but brick is not needed for any upcoming moves. I have 2 wood, but no wood-producing tiles are rolled this turn. I have 1 sheep and 1 ore, but sheep is not needed and ore is not critical. Discarding wheat is the best option as it's a resource I don't need.
Need to reduce hand size due to the 7 rolled. Wood is the most abundant resource in hand and discarding it will not impact future turns as much as the others.

The robber should be moved to block the opponent's resource production. The opponent has settlements on nodes 25 and 28, which produce sheep and brick respectively. Moving the robber to node (2, -1, 0) will block the sheep production from node 25.

The opponent is close to victory and has no resources in hand. Trading for wheat or ore would be beneficial, but BRICK is more valuable. Trading 4 BRICK for 1 WHEAT or 1 ORE would give more resources, helping to reach 10 VP faster. The 3:1 port is available for BRICK.

The opponent has a strong road and is close to completing the longest road. I should end my turn to avoid giving the opponent an opportunity to complete their longest road. Additionally, my hand is not useful for trading or building, and I have no development cards to play.

Need to discard a resource card due to rolling a 7. Wood is the only resource I have, and discarding it will not help me gain any advantages in the current turn.
